# Accept documented Micro-Delta PR reviews in manage_pr_review

## Context

While re-reviewing PR #13900 on 2026-06-23, the Review-Loop Cost Circuit Breaker fired (`35,441` discussion bytes, `4` formal reviews). The pr-review circuit-breaker payload says state `(a)` semantic-cleared loops should use the **Micro-Delta Review Template** from `.agents/skills/pr-review/audits/review-cost-circuit-breaker.md`.

I followed that documented Micro-Delta shape first. `manage_pr_review` rejected it with `PR_REVIEW_TEMPLATE_VALIDATION_FAILED`, saying a recognized anchor like `[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]` was missing. I had to re-post the same substance using the heavier follow-up review template to satisfy the validator.

This is not a reviewer-content issue; it is a documented-template/tool-boundary mismatch.

Release classification: post-release / boardless (review-tooling hardening; workaround exists via the follow-up template, so this should not block release).

## The Problem

`manage_pr_review` mechanically validates every formal review body against anchors for the full/follow-up templates. That is correct for ordinary reviews, but it currently rejects the documented Micro-Delta template that the circuit-breaker itself requires when a long review loop has converged to metadata/mechanical residue.

The effect is negative ROI under exactly the circumstance the circuit-breaker exists to solve:

- reviewers are told to use Micro-Delta to compress a high-cost loop;
- the formal-review tool rejects the Micro-Delta body;
- reviewers must inflate the review back into the full follow-up structure just to flip GitHub review state;
- the cost-compression path is therefore documented but not executable through the canonical `manage_pr_review` surface.

Empirical trigger: PR #13900 re-review at head `3be2313e`; first `manage_pr_review` attempt with `# Pull Request Micro-Delta Review` failed validation, second attempt with `# PR Review Follow-Up Summary` succeeded.

## The Architectural Reality

The mismatch sits between three committed substrates:

- `.agents/skills/pr-review/audits/review-cost-circuit-breaker.md` defines `## Micro-Delta Review Template` for state `(a)` cost-compression.
- `.agents/skills/pr-review/SKILL.md` explicitly says the circuit breaker can classify semantics-cleared loops to `micro-delta`.
- `ai/services/github-workflow/PullRequestService.mjs` validates `manage_pr_review` bodies using visible/invisible template anchors and premise-snapshot requirements; the validation error points only at the full/follow-up template paths.

Tests already protect strict rejection for low-signal bodies and Goodhart-stuffed metric anchors in `test/playwright/unit/ai/services/github-workflow/PullRequestService.spec.mjs`. The fix must preserve those protections while admitting the documented Micro-Delta skeleton only when it matches the circuit-breaker template.

## The Fix

Teach the PR-review validator one of these equivalent shapes:

1. Accept the documented Micro-Delta skeleton as a third valid template class, with its own required anchors (`# Pull Request Micro-Delta Review`, `### State Vector`, `### Micro-Delta Focus`, `### Verdict`, plus enough state fields to keep graph extraction useful); or
2. Update the circuit-breaker documentation to route state `(a)` through the canonical follow-up template instead of advertising a separate Micro-Delta template.

Option 1 is preferred because it preserves the cost-compression intent that motivated the circuit breaker.

Likely code/test surfaces:

- `ai/services/github-workflow/PullRequestService.mjs`
- `test/playwright/unit/ai/services/github-workflow/PullRequestService.spec.mjs`
- `.agents/skills/pr-review/audits/review-cost-circuit-breaker.md` only if the accepted Micro-Delta contract needs tightening

## Contract Ledger Matrix

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `manage_pr_review` review-body validator | `.agents/skills/pr-review/audits/review-cost-circuit-breaker.md` + `PullRequestService.mjs` | Accept documented Micro-Delta reviews without weakening full/follow-up validation | Continue to reject low-signal, Goodhart-stuffed, or structurally incomplete bodies | Circuit-breaker payload remains the caller-facing template authority | Unit test: valid Micro-Delta body succeeds; invalid Micro-Delta/anchor-stuffed body still fails |
| Review-loop cost-compression workflow | pr-review skill + circuit-breaker payload | State `(a)` can be posted as an atomic formal review through `manage_pr_review` | If Micro-Delta remains unsupported, docs must explicitly require follow-up template instead | `.agents/skills/pr-review/audits/review-cost-circuit-breaker.md` | Reproduce with a Micro-Delta fixture modeled on PR #13900’s rejected body |

## Decision Record impact

None. This aligns an MCP tool validator with existing pr-review substrate; it does not amend an ADR.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Add a focused unit test proving a valid `# Pull Request Micro-Delta Review` body passes `PullRequestService.managePrReview()` validation.
- [ ] Add a focused unit test proving an incomplete or Goodhart-stuffed Micro-Delta-like body still fails before any GitHub GraphQL mutation.
- [ ] Preserve the existing full/follow-up strict-template tests.
- [ ] Ensure the validation failure message points to the circuit-breaker Micro-Delta payload when the body clearly attempts Micro-Delta shape and fails that shape.
- [ ] Either keep the Micro-Delta template in `.agents/skills/pr-review/audits/review-cost-circuit-breaker.md` executable as documented, or deliberately retire it from the payload and route state `(a)` to the follow-up template.

## Out of Scope

- Reworking the whole PR-review template system.
- Weakening the anti-Goodhart visible/invisible anchor guard.
- Changing `lint-pr-review-body` CI unless the same validator helper is shared there and must be kept in sync.

## Avoided Traps

- Do not bypass validation for Micro-Delta reviews wholesale. The regression is not that validation exists; it is that one documented template class is absent from the accepted skeletons.
- Do not solve by stuffing metric tags into the Micro-Delta template if that recreates the high-cost follow-up body the circuit-breaker was meant to avoid.
